summary: Bangladesh faces mounting economic pressures as the International Monetary Fund prepares post-Eid negotiations over loan tranches and reform conditions, while the government pursues World Bank partnership and seeks to defer its graduation from least-developed country status. Domestically, a major police and RAB leadership reshuffle reflects institutional restructuring, and ongoing July uprising lawsuits reveal disputes over arbitrary case filings driven by personal conflicts rather than legal substance. Banking sector changes include leadership transitions at major institutions, while the government grapples with price controls as a potential Iran conflict threatens to impact up to three percent of GDP. On a positive note, Bangladesh secured an ODI series victory over Pakistan and launched a nationwide canal excavation programme to improve water management infrastructure.
